meta.title: MS-PBI-5: Data Prep, Modeling, and Managed Delivery

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A Power Query transformation should be executed by the source whenever possible to keep refreshes efficient. Which concept matters most?
questions.explanation: Query folding is correct because supported transformation logic can be pushed to the source system for better refresh efficiency. Bookmarks, RLS, and cross-highlighting solve different report concerns.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A semantic model should be easy to understand and performant for analytics. Which design pattern is recommended?

options.text: One giant denormalized table for every scenario
A separate copy of each dimension for every page
A star schema with fact and dimension tables

questions.explanation: A star schema is correct because Microsoft recommends it for semantic-model clarity and performance. The alternatives create unnecessary ambiguity or maintenance burden.
questions.reference: [Understand star schema and the importance for Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/star-schema)

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A DAX expression must change how a measure is evaluated under additional filters. Which function is central to that pattern?
questions.explanation: CALCULATE is correct because it evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, which is fundamental in DAX. The other functions serve narrower formatting or relationship tasks.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A table is too large for full historical refresh each time. Which feature lets recent partitions refresh more often than old ones?
questions.explanation: Incremental refresh is correct because it refreshes only defined recent periods instead of reprocessing all historical data every time. Bookmarks and visuals do not solve dataset-refresh partitioning.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A report should let users select part of one visual and see related portions emphasized in another visual on the same page. What interaction is this?
questions.explanation: Cross-highlighting is correct because it emphasizes related data in other visuals after a selection while retaining broader context. Gateways, dataflows, and labels are unrelated.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A dataset must restrict rows so regional managers see only their own territory. Which security approach fits best?
questions.explanation: Row-level security is correct because it filters data per user or role within the same semantic model. Formatting, folding, and visuals do not enforce row authorization.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A relationship should let filters flow from a dimension to a fact table in the common case. Which cardinality pattern is most typical in a star schema?

options.text: Many-to-one from fact to dimension as the only valid option

questions.explanation: One-to-many from dimension to fact is correct because star schemas usually model unique dimension keys connected to repeated fact rows. Many-to-many and no-relationship patterns are not the usual default.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A report needs near-real-time queries against the source instead of importing data into the model. Which storage mode is the closest fit?
questions.explanation: DirectQuery is correct because it leaves data at the source and sends queries at interaction time. Import mode caches data in the model, while bookmarks and drillthrough are report features.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A report page needs a dedicated experience that shows detailed records after the user selects a summary data point. Which feature is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Drillthrough is correct because it navigates users to a details page with the selected context applied. Query folding, refresh design, and gateway failover are unrelated.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A team wants a structured promotion path from development to test to production for Power BI artifacts. Which service capability is designed for this?
questions.explanation: Deployment pipelines are correct because they support staged promotion of Power BI content across environments. Quick measures, formatting, and Analyze in Excel do not manage release flow.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. Which Power Query behavior pushes supported transformations back to the source system?
questions.explanation: Query folding is correct because supported transformation logic can be pushed to the source system for better refresh efficiency. Bookmarks, RLS, and cross-highlighting solve different report concerns.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. What modeling approach does Microsoft generally recommend for Power BI semantic models?

options.text: A star schema with fact and dimension tables
One giant denormalized table for every scenario
A separate copy of each dimension for every page

questions.explanation: A star schema is correct because Microsoft recommends it for semantic-model clarity and performance. The alternatives create unnecessary ambiguity or maintenance burden.
questions.reference: [Understand star schema and the importance for Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/star-schema)

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. Which DAX function is commonly used to evaluate an expression in a modified filter context?
questions.explanation: CALCULATE is correct because it evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, which is fundamental in DAX. The other functions serve narrower formatting or relationship tasks.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. What Power BI capability is designed to reduce refresh duration for large fact tables by refreshing only recent data?
questions.explanation: Incremental refresh is correct because it refreshes only defined recent periods instead of reprocessing all historical data every time. Bookmarks and visuals do not solve dataset-refresh partitioning.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. Selecting a bar should highlight related segments elsewhere in the report. Which default Power BI behavior is being used?
questions.explanation: Cross-highlighting is correct because it emphasizes related data in other visuals after a selection while retaining broader context. Gateways, dataflows, and labels are unrelated.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. What should be implemented when different users need different row visibility within the same report?
questions.explanation: Row-level security is correct because it filters data per user or role within the same semantic model. Formatting, folding, and visuals do not enforce row authorization.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. In a standard sales model, one product row relates to many sales rows. What relationship pattern is expected?

options.text: Many-to-one from fact to dimension as the only valid option

questions.explanation: One-to-many from dimension to fact is correct because star schemas usually model unique dimension keys connected to repeated fact rows. Many-to-many and no-relationship patterns are not the usual default.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. If data should stay at the source and queries should run there at report time, which storage mode is appropriate?
questions.explanation: DirectQuery is correct because it leaves data at the source and sends queries at interaction time. Import mode caches data in the model, while bookmarks and drillthrough are report features.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. Users should right-click a visual and move to a focused details page with context passed through. What feature provides this?
questions.explanation: Drillthrough is correct because it navigates users to a details page with the selected context applied. Query folding, refresh design, and gateway failover are unrelated.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. Which feature helps teams move content through staged environments with governance?
questions.explanation: Deployment pipelines are correct because they support staged promotion of Power BI content across environments. Quick measures, formatting, and Analyze in Excel do not manage release flow.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A report author wants efficient refreshes by having the source perform supported shaping steps. What concept is this?
questions.explanation: Query folding is correct because supported transformation logic can be pushed to the source system for better refresh efficiency. Bookmarks, RLS, and cross-highlighting solve different report concerns.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A team is redesigning a model for usability and performance. Which structure should they prefer?

options.text: A separate copy of each dimension for every page
A star schema with fact and dimension tables
One giant denormalized table for every scenario

questions.explanation: A star schema is correct because Microsoft recommends it for semantic-model clarity and performance. The alternatives create unnecessary ambiguity or maintenance burden.
questions.reference: [Understand star schema and the importance for Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/guidance/star-schema)

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A candidate is asked which DAX function changes filter context while computing a measure. What is the best answer?
questions.explanation: CALCULATE is correct because it evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, which is fundamental in DAX. The other functions serve narrower formatting or relationship tasks.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A semantic model needs partition-aware refresh behavior for changing recent periods. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: Incremental refresh is correct because it refreshes only defined recent periods instead of reprocessing all historical data every time. Bookmarks and visuals do not solve dataset-refresh partitioning.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A page needs interactive emphasis across visuals without fully filtering out other values. What is the feature called?
questions.explanation: Cross-highlighting is correct because it emphasizes related data in other visuals after a selection while retaining broader context. Gateways, dataflows, and labels are unrelated.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A report will be shared widely, but each viewer must see only authorized rows. Which feature is designed for that?
questions.explanation: Row-level security is correct because it filters data per user or role within the same semantic model. Formatting, folding, and visuals do not enforce row authorization.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A dimension contains unique keys while the fact table repeats them. Which relationship type is usually correct?

options.text: Many-to-one from fact to dimension as the only valid option

questions.explanation: One-to-many from dimension to fact is correct because star schemas usually model unique dimension keys connected to repeated fact rows. Many-to-many and no-relationship patterns are not the usual default.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A Power BI author wants source-side queries rather than an imported cache. Which mode should be considered first?
questions.explanation: DirectQuery is correct because it leaves data at the source and sends queries at interaction time. Import mode caches data in the model, while bookmarks and drillthrough are report features.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A summary chart should open a filtered detail page for investigation. Which Power BI feature fits?
questions.explanation: Drillthrough is correct because it navigates users to a details page with the selected context applied. Query folding, refresh design, and gateway failover are unrelated.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A report lifecycle requires controlled promotion across environments. What should administrators use?
questions.explanation: Deployment pipelines are correct because they support staged promotion of Power BI content across environments. Quick measures, formatting, and Analyze in Excel do not manage release flow.
